title: Conjecture 1
statement_text: If $G = (V, E)$ is a connected graph embedded in a fixed surface, then $\mathrm{xc}(P_{\mathrm{sp.trees}}(G)) = O(|V|)$.
context_text: The authors prove an $O(g^{1/2}|V|^{3/2} + g^{3/2}|V|^{1/2})$ upper bound for graphs embedded in a surface of genus $g$ (Theorem 3). They conjecture that this can be improved to match the linear bound known for planar graphs (Theorem 2, due to Williams).
paper_title: Smaller Extended Formulations for the Spanning Tree Polytope of Bounded-genus Graphs

title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: If $\mathcal{C}$ is a proper minor-closed family of graphs and $G = (V, E)$ is a connected graph in $\mathcal{C}$, then $\mathrm{xc}(P_{\mathrm{sp.trees}}(G)) = O(|V|)$.
context_text: Following Conjecture 1, the authors suggest the linear bound may hold even more generally for all proper minor-closed families. They note the conjecture is known to hold when graphs in $\mathcal{C}$ have bounded treewidth, and they prove it for $k$-apex graphs (Theorem 4) as additional supporting evidence.
paper_title: Smaller Extended Formulations for the Spanning Tree Polytope of Bounded-genus Graphs
